chunk_summary: This section explores pediatric CKD and CLD nutrition decisions, focusing on post-transplant weight management and refeeding syndrome risks, emphasizing structured interventions and monitoring for metabolic and electrolyte complications.
learning_objectives: Understand nutrition strategies for pediatric CKD and CLD post-transplant cases.
Identify and manage refeeding syndrome risks in children with organ disease and severe malnutrition.
Apply structured meal planning and activity targets to prevent metabolic complications.
Integrate safety-first approaches to balance growth and symptom constraints.
key_facts: Post-transplant rapid weight gain is common with steroids and calcineurin inhibitors; avoid severe calorie/protein restriction or unrestricted sugary foods.
Refeeding syndrome risk increases with cholestasis and edema; monitor phosphate, potassium, magnesium, and glucose closely.
Structured meals and activity targets help prevent metabolic syndrome complications in immunosuppressed children.
Calorie-dense feeds and early tube feeding are recommended for unreliable intake in high-risk malnutrition cases.
Avoid large low-calorie fluid volumes in refeeding syndrome to prevent dangerous electrolyte shifts.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: ASPEN consensus recommendations emphasize close monitoring of phosphate, potassium, and glucose during refeeding syndrome management in malnourished children with organ disease.
document_key_topics: Post-transplant nutrition in pediatric CKD and CLD
Refeeding syndrome risk in severe malnutrition
Structured interventions for metabolic and electrolyte management
